This article is about the area surrounding New York City. For other such areas in the state, see [New York State metropolitan areas](/wiki/New_York_statistical_areas "New York statistical areas").

The **New York metropolitan area**, also called the **Tri-State area** and sometimes referred to as **Greater New York** or **Metro New York**, is the [largest metropolitan economy in the world](/wiki/List_of_cities_by_GDP "List of cities by GDP"), with a [gross metropolitan product](/wiki/List_of_U.S._metropolitan_areas_by_GDP "List of U.S. metropolitan areas by GDP") of over US $2.6 trillion.[[10]](#cite_note-fred.stlouisfed.org-10) It is also the [largest](/wiki/List_of_largest_cities_by_area "List of largest cities by area") [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area") in the world by [urban landmass](/wiki/Urban_area "Urban area"),[[11]](#cite_note-NYCMetroLargestUrbanLandmassWorld1-11)[[12]](#cite_note-NYCMetroLargestUrbanLandmassWorld2-12) encompassing 4,669.0 mi2 (12,093 km2).[[13]](#cite_note-NewYorkCityWorldsLargestUrbanLandmass-13) Among the [most populous metro areas in the world](/wiki/List_of_largest_cities#Metropolitan_area "List of largest cities"), New York is the largest [metropolitan statistical area](/wiki/Metropolitan_statistical_area "Metropolitan statistical area") in the United States and the only one with more than 20 million residents, according to the [2020 U.S. Census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").

The core of this vast area, the New York metropolitan statistical area, includes [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and much of [Downstate New York](/wiki/Downstate_New_York "Downstate New York") ([Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island") as well as the mid- and lower [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley")), [northern](/wiki/North_Jersey "North Jersey") and [central](/wiki/Central_Jersey "Central Jersey") [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey") (including that state's [eleven largest municipalities](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_in_New_Jersey "List of municipalities in New Jersey")), and [Southwestern Connecticut](/wiki/Southwestern_Connecticut "Southwestern Connecticut"). The phrase **Tri-State area** is used to refer to the larger urbanized area of Downstate New York, northern New Jersey, and western Connecticut. The urban region's [combined statistical area](/wiki/Combined_statistical_area "Combined statistical area"), the New York–Newark, NY–NJ–CT–PA combined statistical area, spans four states.

The New York metropolitan statistical area was in 2020 the most populous in the United States, with 20.1 million residents, or slightly over 6% of the nation's total population.[[8]](#cite_note-PopEstCBSA-8) The [combined statistical area](/wiki/Combined_statistical_area#List_of_combined_statistical_areas "Combined statistical area") includes 23.6 million residents as of 2020.[[14]](#cite_note-CityPopulation.de-14)[[15]](#cite_note-CombinedEst-15) It is one of the largest urban agglomerations in the world.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) The New York metropolitan area continues to be the premier gateway for legal [immigration to the United States](/wiki/Immigration_to_the_United_States "Immigration to the United States"),[[19]](#cite_note-Immigrants2013est-19)[[20]](#cite_note-Immigrants2012est-20)[[21]](#cite_note-Immigrants2011est-21)[[22]](#cite_note-Immigrants2010est-22) having the largest [foreign-born population of any metropolitan region](/wiki/Foreign_born#Metropolitan_and_urban_regions_with_the_largest_foreign-born_populations "Foreign born") in the world. The metropolitan statistical area covers 6,140 sq mi (15,903 km2) while the combined statistical area is 13,318 sq mi (34,493 km2), encompassing an ethnically and geographically diverse region. The New York metropolitan area's population is larger than that of the state of New York, and the metropolitan [airspace](/wiki/Airspace "Airspace") accommodated over 130 million passengers in 2016.[[23]](#cite_note-NYMetroAirspace-23)

Greater New York, known as the [financial capital](/wiki/Financial_centre "Financial centre") of the world, is also the hub of several industries, including [health care](/wiki/Health_care "Health care"), [pharmaceuticals](/wiki/List_of_biotech_and_pharmaceutical_companies_in_the_New_York_metropolitan_area "List of biotech and pharmaceutical companies in the New York metropolitan area"), and [scientific output in life sciences](/wiki/List_of_cities_by_scientific_output#Leading_cities_in_different_fields "List of cities by scientific output"),[[24]](#cite_note-NYCLifeSciencesCapital-24)[[25]](#cite_note-NYCHealthCareLifeSciencesHub-25) [international trade](/wiki/International_trade "International trade"), publishing, real estate, education, fashion, [entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment#Industry "Entertainment"), tourism, law, and manufacturing; and if the New York metropolitan area were an independent [sovereign state](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state"), it would constitute the [eighth-largest economy](/wiki/List_of_countries_by_GDP_(nominal) "List of countries by GDP (nominal)") in the world. According to some, it is the most prominent financial,[[26]](#cite_note-NYCDominantFinancialCenter-26)[[27]](#cite_note-WorldEconomicAndFinancialSuperCenter-27)[[28]](#cite_note-Richard_Florida-28) [diplomatic](/wiki/United_Nations_Headquarters "United Nations Headquarters"), and [media](/wiki/Media_in_New_York_City "Media in New York City") hub[[29]](#cite_note-Felix_Richter-29)[[30]](#cite_note-Dawn_Ennis-30) in the world.[[27]](#cite_note-WorldEconomicAndFinancialSuperCenter-27)[[31]](#cite_note-EconomicallyPowerful2015-31)

According to *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")*, in 2014, the New York metropolitan area was home to eight of the top ten [ZIP Codes](/wiki/ZIP_Code "ZIP Code") in the United States by median housing price, with six in [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") alone.[[32]](#cite_note-32) The New York metropolitan area is known for its varied landscape and natural beauty, and contains five of the top ten richest places in America, according to [Bloomberg](/wiki/Bloomberg_L.P. "Bloomberg L.P."). These are [Scarsdale, New York](/wiki/Scarsdale,_New_York "Scarsdale, New York"); [Short Hills, New Jersey](/wiki/Short_Hills,_New_Jersey "Short Hills, New Jersey"); [Old Greenwich, Connecticut](/wiki/Old_Greenwich,_Connecticut "Old Greenwich, Connecticut"); [Bronxville, New York](/wiki/Bronxville,_New_York "Bronxville, New York"); and [Darien, Connecticut](/wiki/Darien,_Connecticut "Darien, Connecticut").[[33]](#cite_note-33) The New York metropolitan region's [higher education network](/wiki/Higher_education "Higher education") comprises hundreds of colleges and universities, including campuses of four [Ivy League](/wiki/Ivy_League "Ivy League") [universities](/wiki/University "University"): [Columbia](/wiki/Columbia_University "Columbia University"), [Princeton](/wiki/Princeton_University "Princeton University"), [Yale](/wiki/Yale_University "Yale University"), and [Cornell](/wiki/Cornell_University "Cornell University") (at [Cornell Tech](/wiki/Cornell_Tech "Cornell Tech") and [Weill Cornell Medicine](/wiki/Weill_Cornell_Medicine "Weill Cornell Medicine")); the flagship campuses of public universities systems at [Stony Brook](/wiki/Stony_Brook_University "Stony Brook University") ([SUNY](/wiki/State_University_of_New_York "State University of New York")), [Rutgers](/wiki/Rutgers_University "Rutgers University") (New Jersey), [New Jersey Institute of Technology](/wiki/New_Jersey_Institute_of_Technology "New Jersey Institute of Technology"); and globally-ranked [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University"), [Rockefeller University](/wiki/Rockefeller_University "Rockefeller University"), and [Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory](/wiki/Cold_Spring_Harbor_Laboratory "Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory").

## Definitions

### Metropolitan statistical area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Metropolitan statistical area")]

Enveloped by the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") and [Long Island Sound](/wiki/Long_Island_Sound "Long Island Sound"), New York City and [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island") are home to approximately 11 million residents combined

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/New_York_Combined_Metro_Area.png/250px-New_York_Combined_Metro_Area.png)](/wiki/File:New_York_Combined_Metro_Area.png)

The U.S. [Office of Management and Budget](/wiki/Office_of_Management_and_Budget "Office of Management and Budget") utilizes two definitions of the urbanized area: the metropolitan statistical area (MSA), and the combined statistical area (CSA). The MSA definition is titled the *New York–Newark–Jersey City, NY–NJ Metropolitan Statistical Area*, and includes a population of 19.9 million people by 2024 Census estimates, roughly 1 in 17 Americans and nearly 2 million more than the second-place [Los Angeles Metro Area](/wiki/Greater_Los_Angeles "Greater Los Angeles") in the United States.[[34]](#cite_note-34) The metropolitan statistical area is further subdivided into four metropolitan divisions. The 23-[county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") metropolitan statistical area includes 10 counties in [New York State](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") (coextensive with the [five boroughs](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)") of New York, the two remaining counties of [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), and three counties in the [Lower](/wiki/Lower_Hudson "Lower Hudson") [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley")) and 12 counties in [Northern](/wiki/North_Jersey "North Jersey") and [Central](/wiki/Central_Jersey "Central Jersey") [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey").[[35]](#cite_note-35) The largest [urbanized area](/wiki/United_States_urban_area "United States urban area") in the United States is at the heart of the metropolitan area, the *New York–Jersey City–Newark, NY–NJ Urban Area*, which had a land area of 3,248 square miles in 2020 according to the 2020 census.[[36]](#cite_note-36) The New York State portion of the metropolitan area, which includes the five boroughs of New York City, the lower Hudson Valley, and Long Island, accounts for over 65 percent of the state's population.

### Combined statistical area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Combined statistical area")]

[Combined statistical areas](/wiki/Combined_statistical_area "Combined statistical area") (CSAs) group together adjacent [core-based statistical areas](/wiki/Core-based_statistical_area "Core-based statistical area") with a high degree of economic interconnection.[[37]](#cite_note-37) The New York–Newark, NY–NJ–CT–PA Combined Statistical Area had an estimated population of 22.3 million as of 2024.[[38]](#cite_note-38) About one out of every fifteen Americans resides in this region, which includes six additional counties in New York, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania and two [planning regions](/wiki/Planning_regions_in_Connecticut "Planning regions in Connecticut") in Connecticut. This area, less the Pennsylvania portion, is often referred to as the *tri-state area* and less commonly the *tri-state region*. The New York City television [designated market area](/wiki/Media_market "Media market") (DMA) includes [Pike County, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pike_County,_Pennsylvania "Pike County, Pennsylvania"),[[39]](#cite_note-39) which is also included in the CSA.

In addition to the New York–Newark–Jersey City, NY–NJ metropolitan statistical area (MSA), the following core-based statistical areas are also included in the New York–Newark, NY–NJ–CT–PA CSA:

* [Bridgeport–Stamford–Danbury, CT](/wiki/Greater_Bridgeport "Greater Bridgeport") MSA (972,679)
  + [Greater Bridgeport Planning Region](/wiki/Greater_Bridgeport_Planning_Region,_Connecticut "Greater Bridgeport Planning Region, Connecticut")
  + [Western Connecticut Planning Region](/wiki/Western_Connecticut_Planning_Region,_Connecticut "Western Connecticut Planning Region, Connecticut")
* [Hemlock Farms, PA](/wiki/Hemlock_Farms,_Pennsylvania "Hemlock Farms, Pennsylvania") μSA (62,376)[[40]](#cite_note-40)
  + [Pike County](/wiki/Pike_County,_Pennsylvania "Pike County, Pennsylvania")
* [Kingston, NY](/wiki/Kingston,_New_York "Kingston, New York") MSA (182,977)
  + [Ulster County](/wiki/Ulster_County,_New_York "Ulster County, New York")
* [Kiryas Joel–Poughkeepsie–Newburgh, NY](/wiki/Kiryas_Joel%E2%80%93Poughkeepsie%E2%80%93Newburgh_metropolitan_area "Kiryas Joel–Poughkeepsie–Newburgh metropolitan area") MSA (711,730)
  + [Dutchess County](/wiki/Dutchess_County,_New_York "Dutchess County, New York")
  + [Orange County](/wiki/Orange_County,_New_York "Orange County, New York")
* [Monticello, NY](/wiki/Monticello,_New_York "Monticello, New York") MSA (80,450)
  + [Sullivan County](/wiki/Sullivan_County,_New_York "Sullivan County, New York")
* [Trenton](/wiki/Trenton,_New_Jersey "Trenton, New Jersey")–[Princeton, NJ](/wiki/Princeton,_New_Jersey "Princeton, New Jersey") MSA (392,138)
  + [Mercer County](/wiki/Mercer_County,_New_Jersey "Mercer County, New Jersey")

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/High_Point_Monument_and_Lake_Marcia_framed.jpg/250px-High_Point_Monument_and_Lake_Marcia_framed.jpg)](/wiki/File:High_Point_Monument_and_Lake_Marcia_framed.jpg)

[High Point Monument](/wiki/High_Point_Monument "High Point Monument") seen from Lake Marcia at High Point in [Sussex County, New Jersey](/wiki/Sussex_County,_New_Jersey "Sussex County, New Jersey"), the highest elevation in [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey") at 1,803 feet (550 m) above sea level.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/New_York_City_Area_Municipalities.png/250px-New_York_City_Area_Municipalities.png)](/wiki/File:New_York_City_Area_Municipalities.png)

New York City Area [Municipalities](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_in_New_York "List of municipalities in New York")

The area is frequently divided into the following subregions:[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)

* [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") (the primary urban center of the metropolitan region, comprising five [boroughs](/wiki/Boroughs_of_New_York_City "Boroughs of New York City"), one of which is [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), the geographical, cultural, and economic core of the entire metropolitan area)
* Central and eastern [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island") ([Nassau](/wiki/Nassau_County,_New_York "Nassau County, New York") and [Suffolk](/wiki/Suffolk_County,_New_York "Suffolk County, New York") Counties – separated by water from the rest of the region except New York City; not including [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens") County or Kings County ([Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn")), which are concurrent with two of New York's five boroughs)
* [North Jersey](/wiki/North_Jersey "North Jersey") (northern portion of New Jersey)
* [Central Jersey](/wiki/Central_Jersey "Central Jersey") (middle portion of New Jersey)
* [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley") (Lower Hudson Valley suburbs of [Westchester](/wiki/Westchester_County,_New_York "Westchester County, New York"), [Putnam](/wiki/Putnam_County,_New_York "Putnam County, New York"), and [Rockland](/wiki/Rockland_County,_New_York "Rockland County, New York") Counties; and Mid-Hudson [exurbs](/wiki/Exurb "Exurb") of Dutchess, Sullivan, Orange, and Ulster Counties)
* Western Connecticut (Only [Fairfield](/wiki/Fairfield_County,_Connecticut "Fairfield County, Connecticut"), [New Haven](/wiki/New_Haven_County,_Connecticut "New Haven County, Connecticut"), and [Litchfield](/wiki/Litchfield_County,_Connecticut "Litchfield County, Connecticut") Counties are part of the region and separated by the state line)
* Southern and Eastern [Poconos](/wiki/Pocono_Mountains "Pocono Mountains") ([Monroe](/wiki/Monroe_County,_Pennsylvania "Monroe County, Pennsylvania") and [Pike](/wiki/Pike_County,_Pennsylvania "Pike County, Pennsylvania") Counties in Pennsylvania)

All eight subregions are often further subdivided. For instance, Long Island can be divided into its [South](/wiki/South_Shore_(Long_Island) "South Shore (Long Island)") and [North Shores](/wiki/Gold_Coast_(Long_Island) "Gold Coast (Long Island)") (usually when speaking about Nassau County and western Suffolk County) and the [East End](/wiki/East_End_(Long_Island) "East End (Long Island)"). The Hudson Valley and Connecticut are sometimes grouped together and referred to as the Northern Suburbs, largely because of the shared usage of the [Metro-North Railroad](/wiki/Metro-North_Railroad "Metro-North Railroad") system.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Climate")]

Under the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"), New York City, western (and parts of eastern) Long Island, and the Jersey Shore experience a [humid subtropical climate](/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate") (*Cfa*),[[45]](#cite_note-Peel,_M._C.;_Finlayson,_B._L.;_McMahon,_T._A-45)[[46]](#cite_note-newyorkpolonia.com-46) and New York is thus the northernmost major city on the North American continent with this climate type.

Much of the remainder of the metropolitan area lies in the transition zone from a humid subtropical (*Cfa*) to a [humid continental climate](/wiki/Humid_continental_climate "Humid continental climate") (*Dfa*),[[45]](#cite_note-Peel,_M._C.;_Finlayson,_B._L.;_McMahon,_T._A-45)[[46]](#cite_note-newyorkpolonia.com-46) and it is only the inland, more exurban areas far to the north and west such as Sussex County, New Jersey, that have a January daily average of −3 °C (26.6 °F) or below and are fully humid continental; the *Dfb* (warm summer subtype) regime is only found inland at a higher elevation,[[45]](#cite_note-Peel,_M._C.;_Finlayson,_B._L.;_McMahon,_T._A-45) and receives greater snowfall[[47]](#cite_note-47) than the *Dfa* region. Much of Monroe and most of Pike County in Pennsylvania also have a fully humid continental climate.

Summers in the area are typically hot and humid. Nighttime conditions in and around the five boroughs of New York are often exacerbated by the [urban heat island](/wiki/Urban_heat_island "Urban heat island") phenomenon, and temperatures exceed 90 °F (32 °C) on average of 7–8 days (on the immediate [Long Island Sound](/wiki/Long_Island_Sound "Long Island Sound") and Atlantic coasts), up to in excess of 27 days (inland suburbs in New Jersey) each summer and may exceed 100 °F (38 °C).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Normally, warm to hot temperatures begin in mid-May, and last through early October. Summers also feature passing thundershowers which build in the heat of the day and then drop brief, but intense, rainfall.

Winters are cold with a mix of rain and snow. Although prevailing winds in winter are offshore, and temper the moderating effects of the Atlantic Ocean, the Atlantic and the partial shielding by the [Appalachians](/wiki/Appalachians "Appalachians") from colder air keep the New York area warmer in the winter than inland North American metropolitan areas located at similar or lesser latitudes including [Pittsburgh](/wiki/Pittsburgh "Pittsburgh"), [Cincinnati](/wiki/Cincinnati "Cincinnati"), and [Indianapolis](/wiki/Indianapolis "Indianapolis"). Warm periods with 50 °F (10 °C)+ temperatures may occasionally occur during winter as well.[[48]](#cite_note-48) The [hardiness zone](/wiki/Hardiness_zone "Hardiness zone") in the New York metropolitan area varies over a wide range from 5a in the highest areas of Dutchess, Monroe, and Ulster Counties to 7b in most of NYC as well as Hudson County from Bayonne up the east side of the Palisades to Route 495, the majority of Nassau County, the north coast of Monmouth County, and Copiague Harbor, Lindenhurst, and Montauk in Suffolk County.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

Almost all of the metropolitan area receives at least 42 inches (1,070 mm) of precipitation annually, which is relatively evenly spread throughout the year, and many areas receive upwards of 50 in (1,270 mm). Average winter snowfall for 1981 to 2010 ranges from just under 25 inches (64 cm) along the coast of Long Island to more than 50 in (127 cm) in some inland areas, but this usually varies considerably from year to year.[[50]](#cite_note-Weatherbase_New_York,_New_York-50) Hurricanes and tropical storms have impacted the Tri-State area in the past, though a direct hit is rare. Several areas on Long Island, New Jersey, and the Connecticut coast have been impacted by serious [storm surges](/wiki/Storm_surge "Storm surge") in the past. Inland areas have been impacted by heavy rain and flooding from tropical cyclones.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

The New York metropolitan area averages 234 days with at least some [sunshine](/wiki/Sunshine "Sunshine") and 59% of possible sunlight annually,[[52]](#cite_note-Percent_Sunshine-52) accumulating 2,400 to 2,800 hours of sunshine per annum.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

The geographical, cultural, and economic center of the metropolitan area is New York City, the most populous city in the United States and has been described as the [capital of the world](/wiki/Caput_mundi "Caput mundi").[[64]](#cite_note-NYCTheCapitaloftheWorld-66) The city consists of five [boroughs](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)"), each of which is coterminous with a [county of New York State](/wiki/List_of_counties_in_New_York "List of counties in New York"). The five boroughs – [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens"), [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), the [Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx"), and [Staten Island](/wiki/Staten_Island "Staten Island") – were [consolidated into a single city](/wiki/City_of_Greater_New_York "City of Greater New York") in 1898.[[65]](#cite_note-67) With a Census-estimated [population](/wiki/New_York_City#Demographics "New York City") of 8,335,897 in 2022 (8,467,513 in 2021[[66]](#cite_note-2014NYCest2-68)[[67]](#cite_note-census-est-nyc-ny-69)), distributed over a land area of just 305 square miles (790 km2),[[68]](#cite_note-70) New York is the [most densely populated](/wiki/List_of_United_States_cities_by_population_density "List of United States cities by population density") major city in the United States.[[69]](#cite_note-density-71) A [global power city](/wiki/Global_city "Global city"),[[70]](#cite_note-72) New York City exerts a significant impact upon commerce, finance, [health care](/wiki/Health_care "Health care") and [life sciences](/wiki/Life_sciences "Life sciences"),[[25]](#cite_note-NYCHealthCareLifeSciencesHub-25) media, dining, art, fashion, research, technology, education, and entertainment, its fast pace[[71]](#cite_note-FastPaceNYC1-73)[[72]](#cite_note-74) defining the term *[New York minute](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/New_York_minute "wikt:New York minute")*.[[73]](#cite_note-NewYorkMinuteDefinition-75) Home to the [headquarters of the United Nations](/wiki/Headquarters_of_the_United_Nations "Headquarters of the United Nations"),[[74]](#cite_note-76) New York is an important center for [international diplomacy](/wiki/International_diplomacy "International diplomacy").[[75]](#cite_note-77) New York is a [global city](/wiki/Global_city "Global city")[[76]](#cite_note-78) and has been described as the [cultural](/wiki/Culture_of_New_York_City "Culture of New York City"),[[77]](#cite_note-79)[[78]](#cite_note-culture4-80) [financial](/wiki/Wall_Street "Wall Street"),[[26]](#cite_note-NYCDominantFinancialCenter-26)[[27]](#cite_note-WorldEconomicAndFinancialSuperCenter-27) [entertainment](/wiki/The_Entertainment_Capital_of_the_World "The Entertainment Capital of the World"),[[64]](#cite_note-NYCTheCapitaloftheWorld-66) and [media](/wiki/Media_in_New_York_City "Media in New York City") capital[[29]](#cite_note-Felix_Richter-29)[[30]](#cite_note-Dawn_Ennis-30) of the world, as well as the world's most economically powerful city.[[31]](#cite_note-EconomicallyPowerful2015-31)[[27]](#cite_note-WorldEconomicAndFinancialSuperCenter-27)[[79]](#cite_note-81)[[28]](#cite_note-Richard_Florida-28)

### Long Island

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Long Island")]

Main article: [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ab/Pineapple_-_Garden_City_NY.jpg/250px-Pineapple_-_Garden_City_NY.jpg)](/wiki/File:Pineapple_-_Garden_City_NY.jpg)

A welcome sign in [Garden City](/wiki/Garden_City,_New_York "Garden City, New York") in [Nassau County](/wiki/Nassau_County,_New_York "Nassau County, New York") on [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/WesthamptonInnerWaterway.jpg/250px-WesthamptonInnerWaterway.jpg)](/wiki/File:WesthamptonInnerWaterway.jpg)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/John_Miller_Great_Neck_North_High_School%2C_Great_Neck%2C_NY_August_14%2C_2022.jpg/250px-John_Miller_Great_Neck_North_High_School%2C_Great_Neck%2C_NY_August_14%2C_2022.jpg)](/wiki/File:John_Miller_Great_Neck_North_High_School,_Great_Neck,_NY_August_14,_2022.jpg)

[Great Neck North High School](/wiki/John_L._Miller_Great_Neck_North_High_School "John L. Miller Great Neck North High School") in [Great Neck](/wiki/Great_Neck_(village),_New_York "Great Neck (village), New York"), [Nassau County](/wiki/Nassau_County,_New_York "Nassau County, New York")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/OHEKA_CASTLE_exterior_view_3.jpg/250px-OHEKA_CASTLE_exterior_view_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:OHEKA_CASTLE_exterior_view_3.jpg)

The [Otto Kahn Estate](/wiki/Oheka_Castle "Oheka Castle") on Long Island's historic [North Shore](/wiki/North_Shore_(Long_Island) "North Shore (Long Island)"). This [Gilded Age](/wiki/Gilded_Age "Gilded Age") estate served as partial inspiration for the *"Gatsby estate"* in [F. Scott Fitzgerald](/wiki/F._Scott_Fitzgerald "F. Scott Fitzgerald")'s *[The Great Gatsby](/wiki/The_Great_Gatsby "The Great Gatsby")*.[[80]](#cite_note-82)

[Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), the most populous island in the United States, is located just off the northeast coast of the United States and is a region wholly included within both the U.S. state of New York and the New York metropolitan area. Extending 118 miles east-northeast of [Roosevelt Island, Manhattan](/wiki/Roosevelt_Island,_Manhattan "Roosevelt Island, Manhattan") from [New York Harbor](/wiki/New_York_Harbor "New York Harbor") into the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), the island comprises four counties: Kings and Queens (these form the New York [boroughs](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") and [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens"), respectively) to the west; then [Nassau](/wiki/Nassau_County,_New_York "Nassau County, New York") and [Suffolk](/wiki/Suffolk_County,_New_York "Suffolk County, New York") to the east. However, most people in the New York metropolitan area (even those living in Queens and Brooklyn) colloquially use the term "Long Island" (or "The Island") exclusively to refer to Nassau County and Suffolk County collectively, which are mainly suburban in character.[[81]](#cite_note-83) North of the island is [Long Island Sound](/wiki/Long_Island_Sound "Long Island Sound"), across which are the U.S. states of [Connecticut](/wiki/Connecticut "Connecticut") and [Rhode Island](/wiki/Rhode_Island "Rhode Island").

With a population of 8,063,232 enumerated at the [2020 U.S. Census](/wiki/2020_U.S._Census "2020 U.S. Census"), constituting nearly 40% of New York State's population,[[82]](#cite_note-census-est-ny-84)[[83]](#cite_note-Kings_County,_New_York_QuickFacts-85)[[84]](#cite_note-Queens_County,_New_York_QuickFacts-86)[[85]](#cite_note-Nassau_County,_New_York_QuickFacts-87)[[86]](#cite_note-U.S._Census_Bureau-88) the majority of New York City residents, 58.4% as of 2020, live on Long Island, namely the estimated 4,896,398 residents living in the [New York City boroughs](/wiki/Borough_of_New_York_City "Borough of New York City") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") and [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens").[[87]](#cite_note-LINYCpopulation-89) Long Island is the [most populated](/wiki/List_of_islands_by_population "List of islands by population") island in any U.S. state or territory, and the 17th-most populous island in the world (ahead of [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland"), [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica"), and [Hokkaidō](/wiki/Hokkaido "Hokkaido")). Its [population density](/wiki/List_of_islands_by_population_density "List of islands by population density") is 5,571 inhabitants per square mile (2,151/km2). If Long Island geographically constituted an independent metropolitan statistical area, it would rank [fourth most populous](/wiki/List_of_Metropolitan_Statistical_Areas "List of Metropolitan Statistical Areas") in the United States; while if it were a U.S. state, Long Island would rank [13th in population](/wiki/List_of_U.S._states_by_population "List of U.S. states by population") and [first in population density](/wiki/List_of_U.S._states_by_population_density "List of U.S. states by population density"). Queens is the most [ethnically diverse](/wiki/Ethnic_diversity "Ethnic diversity") urban area in the world.[[88]](#cite_note-90)[[89]](#cite_note-91) The [Town of Hempstead](/wiki/Hempstead,_New_York "Hempstead, New York") in Nassau County, with an estimated population of 770,367 in 2016, is the most populous municipality in the New York metropolitan area outside of New York City.[[90]](#cite_note-HempsteadPopulation-92)

Long Island is also the [17th most populous island in the world](/wiki/List_of_islands_by_population "List of islands by population"), but is more prominently known for recreation, boating, and miles of public beaches, including numerous town, county, and state parks, as well as [Fire Island National Seashore](/wiki/Fire_Island_National_Seashore "Fire Island National Seashore") and wealthy and expensive coastal residential enclaves. Along the north shore, the [Gold Coast of Long Island](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Long_Island "Gold Coast, Long Island"), featured in the film *[The Great Gatsby](/wiki/The_Great_Gatsby_(2013_film) "The Great Gatsby (2013 film)")*, is an upscale section of Nassau and western Suffolk counties that once featured many lavish mansions built and inhabited by wealthy business [tycoons](/wiki/Tycoon "Tycoon") in the earlier years of the 20th century, of which only a few remain preserved as historic sites. The [East End](/wiki/East_End_(Long_Island) "East End (Long Island)") of Long Island (known as the "Twin Forks" because of its physical shape) boasts open spaces for farmland and [wineries](/wiki/Winery "Winery"). The South Fork, in particular, comprises numerous towns and villages known collectively as "[The Hamptons](/wiki/The_Hamptons "The Hamptons")" and has an international reputation as a "playground for the rich and famous", with some of the wealthiest communities in the United States. In 2015, according to *[Business Insider](/wiki/Business_Insider "Business Insider")*, the 11962 zip code encompassing [Sagaponack](/wiki/Sagaponack,_New_York "Sagaponack, New York"), within [Southampton](/wiki/Southampton,_New_York "Southampton, New York"), was listed as the most expensive in the U.S. by real estate-listings site Property Shark, with a median home sale price of $5,125,000.[[91]](#cite_note-93)

During the summer season, many celebrities and the wealthy visit or reside in mansions and waterfront homes, while others spend weekends enjoying the beaches, gardens, bars, restaurants, and nightclubs.

Long Island is served by a network of [parkways](/wiki/Parkway "Parkway") and [expressways](/wiki/Controlled-access_highway "Controlled-access highway"), with the [Long Island Expressway](/wiki/Long_Island_Expressway "Long Island Expressway"), [Northern State Parkway](/wiki/Northern_State_Parkway "Northern State Parkway"), and [Southern State Parkway](/wiki/Southern_State_Parkway "Southern State Parkway") being major east–west routes across significant portions of the island. [Commuter rail](/wiki/Commuter_rail_in_North_America "Commuter rail in North America") access is provided by the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) [Long Island Rail Road](/wiki/Long_Island_Rail_Road "Long Island Rail Road"), one of the largest commuter railroads in the United States. Air travel needs are served by several airports. Within Queens, the island is home to [John F. Kennedy International Airport](/wiki/John_F._Kennedy_International_Airport "John F. Kennedy International Airport") and [LaGuardia Airport](/wiki/LaGuardia_Airport "LaGuardia Airport"), two of the three major airline hubs serving the New York area (with Newark Liberty International Airport being the third; all three major airports are operated by The Port Authority of New York & New Jersey). [Long Island MacArthur Airport](/wiki/Long_Island_MacArthur_Airport "Long Island MacArthur Airport") (serving commercial airlines) and [Farmingdale/Republic Airport](/wiki/Farmingdale/Republic_Airport "Farmingdale/Republic Airport") (private and commuter flights) are both located in Suffolk County.

### Lower Hudson Valley

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Lower Hudson Valley")]

Main article: [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/Sugarloaf_Hill_Hudson_Highlands_from_Bear_Mountain_Bridge.JPG/250px-Sugarloaf_Hill_Hudson_Highlands_from_Bear_Mountain_Bridge.JPG)](/wiki/File:Sugarloaf_Hill_Hudson_Highlands_from_Bear_Mountain_Bridge.JPG)

[Sugarloaf Hill](/wiki/Sugarloaf_Hill_(Putnam_County,_New_York) "Sugarloaf Hill (Putnam County, New York)") in [Putnam County, New York](/wiki/Putnam_County,_New_York "Putnam County, New York"), in the [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Bear_Mtn_Bridge.jpg/250px-Bear_Mtn_Bridge.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bear_Mtn_Bridge.jpg)

[Bear Mountain Bridge](/wiki/Bear_Mountain_Bridge "Bear Mountain Bridge"), which connects [Westchester](/wiki/Westchester_County "Westchester County") and [Orange](/wiki/Orange_County,_New_York "Orange County, New York") counties in [New York State](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), across the [Hudson River](/wiki/Hudson_River "Hudson River")

Known for its [hilly terrain](/wiki/Topography "Topography"), picturesque settings, and quaint small towns and [villages](/wiki/Village_(New_York) "Village (New York)"), the Lower Hudson Valley is centered around the [Hudson River](/wiki/Hudson_River "Hudson River") north of New York City and lies within New York State. [Westchester](/wiki/Westchester_County,_New_York "Westchester County, New York") and [Putnam](/wiki/Putnam_County,_New_York "Putnam County, New York") counties are located on the eastern side of the river, and [Rockland](/wiki/Rockland_County "Rockland County") and [Orange](/wiki/Orange_County,_New_York "Orange County, New York") counties are located on the western side of the river. Westchester and Rockland counties are connected by the heavily trafficked [New Tappan Zee Bridge](/wiki/Tappan_Zee_Bridge_(2017%E2%80%93present) "Tappan Zee Bridge (2017–present)"), as well as by the [Bear Mountain Bridge](/wiki/Bear_Mountain_Bridge "Bear Mountain Bridge") near their northern ends. Several branches of the [MTA Metro-North Railroad](/wiki/Metro-North_Railroad "Metro-North Railroad") serve the region's rail commuters. [Southern Westchester County](/wiki/Southern_Westchester "Southern Westchester") contains more densely populated areas and includes the cities of [Yonkers](/wiki/Yonkers "Yonkers"), [Mount Vernon](/wiki/Mount_Vernon,_New_York "Mount Vernon, New York"), [New Rochelle](/wiki/New_Rochelle "New Rochelle"), and [White Plains](/wiki/White_Plains,_New_York "White Plains, New York"). Many of the suburban communities of Westchester are known for their affluence and expense (some examples: Bronxville, Scarsdale, Chappaqua, Armonk, Pound Ridge, Katonah, and Briarcliff Manor). Rockland's river towns along the Hudson, including [Nyack](/wiki/Nyack,_New_York "Nyack, New York") and [Piermont](/wiki/Piermont,_New_York "Piermont, New York"), are known for their vibrant dining and art scenes. 30% of Rockland's land area is designated parkland with impressive scenery, which attracts many visitors from the tri-state area. In recent years, the high cost of housing in the Lower Hudson Valley, plus increased remote working opportunities, has caused some to move further north into the Mid Hudson Valley.

Historically, the valley was home to many factories, including paper mills, but a significant number have closed. After years of lingering pollution, cleanup efforts to improve the Hudson River water quality are currently planned and will be supervised by the [United States Environmental Protection Agency](/wiki/United_States_Environmental_Protection_Agency "United States Environmental Protection Agency") (EPA).[[92]](#cite_note-EPA-94)

### Mid-Hudson Valley

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mid-Hudson Valley")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Mohonk_Mountain_House_2011_View_of_Mohonk_Guest_Rooms_from_One_Hiking_Trail_FRD_3205.jpg/250px-Mohonk_Mountain_House_2011_View_of_Mohonk_Guest_Rooms_from_One_Hiking_Trail_FRD_3205.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mohonk_Mountain_House_2011_View_of_Mohonk_Guest_Rooms_from_One_Hiking_Trail_FRD_3205.jpg)

[Mohonk Mountain House](/wiki/Mohonk_Mountain_House "Mohonk Mountain House") in [Ulster County, New York](/wiki/Ulster_County,_New_York "Ulster County, New York"), in the [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley"), was designated a [National Historic Landmark](/wiki/National_Historic_Landmark "National Historic Landmark") in 1986.[[93]](#cite_note-95)

The Mid-Hudson Valley region of the [State of New York](/wiki/State_of_New_York "State of New York") is midway between New York City and the state capital of [Albany](/wiki/Albany,_New_York "Albany, New York"). The area includes the counties of [Dutchess](/wiki/Dutchess_County "Dutchess County"), [Ulster](/wiki/Ulster_County "Ulster County"), and [Sullivan](/wiki/Sullivan_County,_New_York "Sullivan County, New York"), as well as the northern portions of [Orange County](/wiki/Orange_County,_New_York "Orange County, New York"), with the region's main cities being [Poughkeepsie](/wiki/Poughkeepsie,_New_York "Poughkeepsie, New York"), [Newburgh](/wiki/Newburgh,_New_York "Newburgh, New York"), [Kingston](/wiki/Kingston,_New_York "Kingston, New York"), and [Beacon](/wiki/Beacon,_New_York "Beacon, New York"). The [Walkway over the Hudson](/wiki/Walkway_over_the_Hudson "Walkway over the Hudson"), is the second longest pedestrian [footbridge](/wiki/Footbridge "Footbridge") in the world. It crosses the [Hudson River](/wiki/Hudson_River "Hudson River") connecting [Poughkeepsie](/wiki/Poughkeepsie,_New_York "Poughkeepsie, New York") and [Highland](/wiki/Highland,_Ulster_County,_New_York "Highland, Ulster County, New York"). The 13 mile-long [Dutchess Rail Trail](/wiki/Dutchess_Rail_Trail "Dutchess Rail Trail") stretches from [Hopewell Junction](/wiki/Hopewell_Junction,_New_York "Hopewell Junction, New York") to the beginning of the Walkway over the Hudson in Poughkeepsie. The area is home to the [Wappingers Central School District](/wiki/Wappingers_Central_School_District "Wappingers Central School District"), which the second-largest school district in the [state of New York](/wiki/State_of_New_York "State of New York"). The Newburgh Waterfront in the City of [Newburgh](/wiki/Newburgh,_New_York "Newburgh, New York") is home to many high-end restaurants.

[U.S. Route 9](/wiki/U.S._Route_9 "U.S. Route 9"), [I-84](/wiki/Interstate_84_(Pennsylvania%E2%80%93Massachusetts) "Interstate 84 (Pennsylvania–Massachusetts)"), and the [Taconic State Parkway](/wiki/Taconic_State_Parkway "Taconic State Parkway") all run through Dutchess County. [Metro-North Railroad](/wiki/Metro-North_Railroad "Metro-North Railroad") train station, [New Hamburg](/wiki/New_Hamburg,_New_York "New Hamburg, New York"), is located in the Town of [Poughkeepsie](/wiki/Poughkeepsie,_New_York "Poughkeepsie, New York") and runs from [Poughkeepsie](/wiki/Poughkeepsie,_New_York "Poughkeepsie, New York") to [Grand Central Terminal](/wiki/Grand_Central_Terminal "Grand Central Terminal") in New York City.

### Northern New Jersey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Northern New Jersey")]

Main articles: [North Jersey](/wiki/North_Jersey "North Jersey"), [Gateway Region](/wiki/Gateway_Region "Gateway Region"), and [Skylands Region](/wiki/Skylands_Region "Skylands Region")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b3/Bigfalls14w_info.jpg/250px-Bigfalls14w_info.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bigfalls14w_info.jpg)

The [Great Falls](/wiki/Great_Falls_(Passaic_River) "Great Falls (Passaic River)") of the [Passaic River](/wiki/Passaic_River "Passaic River") in [Paterson, New Jersey](/wiki/Paterson,_New_Jersey "Paterson, New Jersey"), which was dedicated as a [National Historical Park](/wiki/Paterson_Great_Falls_National_Historical_Park "Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park") in November 2011, incorporates one of the largest [waterfalls](/wiki/Waterfall "Waterfall") on the [U.S. East Coast](/wiki/East_Coast_of_the_United_States "East Coast of the United States").[[94]](#cite_note-96)

[Northern New Jersey](/wiki/North_Jersey "North Jersey"), also known colloquially as North Jersey, is typically defined as comprising the following counties:

* [Bergen County](/wiki/Bergen_County,_New_Jersey "Bergen County, New Jersey")
* [Essex County](/wiki/Essex_County,_New_Jersey "Essex County, New Jersey")
* [Somerset County](/wiki/Somerset_County,_New_Jersey "Somerset County, New Jersey") (anything north of [Bridgewater Township](/wiki/Bridgewater_Township,_New_Jersey "Bridgewater Township, New Jersey"))
* [Hudson County](/wiki/Hudson_County,_New_Jersey "Hudson County, New Jersey")
* [Hunterdon County](/wiki/Hunterdon_County,_New_Jersey "Hunterdon County, New Jersey") (anything north of [Readington Township](/wiki/Readington_Township,_New_Jersey "Readington Township, New Jersey"))
* [Morris County](/wiki/Morris_County,_New_Jersey "Morris County, New Jersey")
* [Passaic County](/wiki/Passaic_County,_New_Jersey "Passaic County, New Jersey")
* [Sussex County](/wiki/Sussex_County,_New_Jersey "Sussex County, New Jersey")
* [Union County](/wiki/Union_County,_New_Jersey "Union County, New Jersey") (anything north of [Scotch Plains Township](/wiki/Scotch_Plains_Township,_New_Jersey "Scotch Plains Township, New Jersey"), [Clark Township](/wiki/Clark_Township,_New_Jersey "Clark Township, New Jersey"), and [Linden](/wiki/Linden,_New_Jersey "Linden, New Jersey"))
* [Warren County](/wiki/Warren_County,_New_Jersey "Warren County, New Jersey")

The New Jersey State Department of Tourism splits North Jersey into the urban [Gateway Region](/wiki/Gateway_Region "Gateway Region") and the more rural [Skylands Region](/wiki/Skylands_Region "Skylands Region"). Northern New Jersey is home to four of the largest cities of that state: [Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_Jersey "Newark, New Jersey"), [Jersey City](/wiki/Jersey_City,_New_Jersey "Jersey City, New Jersey"), [Paterson](/wiki/Paterson,_New_Jersey "Paterson, New Jersey"), and [Elizabeth](/wiki/Elizabeth,_New_Jersey "Elizabeth, New Jersey").

The region is geographically diverse with [wetlands](/wiki/Wetland "Wetland"), mountains, and valleys throughout the area. It has a large network of [expressways](/wiki/Limited-access_road "Limited-access road") and [public transportation rail](/wiki/Commuter_rail "Commuter rail") services, mostly operated by [New Jersey Transit](/wiki/New_Jersey_Transit "New Jersey Transit"). Northern New Jersey also contains the second busiest airport in the New York metropolitan area, [Newark Liberty International Airport](/wiki/Newark_Liberty_International_Airport "Newark Liberty International Airport").

Although it is a suburban and rural region of [New York](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), much of the [Gateway Region](/wiki/Gateway_Region "Gateway Region") is highly urbanized. The entirety of Hudson County, eastern Essex County, southern Passaic County as well as [Elizabeth](/wiki/Elizabeth,_New_Jersey "Elizabeth, New Jersey") in Union County are all densely populated areas.

[![Pictured The Palisades, with the George Washington Bridge on the left. The Palisades are a line of steep cliffs along the west side of the Hudson River in Northeastern New Jersey and Southeastern New York, stretching north from Jersey City, New Jersey to Nyack, New York.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/NJPalisades.JPG/1920px-NJPalisades.JPG)](/wiki/File:NJPalisades.JPG "Pictured The Palisades, with the George Washington Bridge on the left. The Palisades are a line of steep cliffs along the west side of the Hudson River in Northeastern New Jersey and Southeastern New York, stretching north from Jersey City, New Jersey to Nyack, New York.")

Pictured [The Palisades](/wiki/The_Palisades_(Hudson_River) "The Palisades (Hudson River)"), with the [George Washington Bridge](/wiki/George_Washington_Bridge "George Washington Bridge") on the left. The Palisades are a line of steep cliffs along the west side of the [Hudson River](/wiki/Hudson_River "Hudson River") in [Northeastern New Jersey](/wiki/Gateway_Region "Gateway Region") and [Southeastern New York](/wiki/Downstate_New_York "Downstate New York"), stretching north from [Jersey City, New Jersey](/wiki/Jersey_City,_New_Jersey "Jersey City, New Jersey") to [Nyack, New York](/wiki/Nyack,_New_York "Nyack, New York").

### Central New Jersey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Central New Jersey")]

Main articles: [Central Jersey](/wiki/Central_Jersey "Central Jersey") and [Jersey Shore](/wiki/Jersey_Shore "Jersey Shore")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/93/2009-08-17_View_of_downtown_Trenton_in_New_Jersey_and_the_mouth_of_the_Assunpink_Creek_from_across_the_Delaware_River_in_Morrisville%2C_Pennsylvania.jpg/250px-2009-08-17_View_of_downtown_Trenton_in_New_Jersey_and_the_mouth_of_the_Assunpink_Creek_from_across_the_Delaware_River_in_Morrisville%2C_Pennsylvania.jpg)](/wiki/File:2009-08-17_View_of_downtown_Trenton_in_New_Jersey_and_the_mouth_of_the_Assunpink_Creek_from_across_the_Delaware_River_in_Morrisville,_Pennsylvania.jpg)

Downtown [Trenton](/wiki/Trenton,_New_Jersey "Trenton, New Jersey") in [Mercer County](/wiki/Mercer_County,_New_Jersey "Mercer County, New Jersey"), including the [New Jersey State House](/wiki/New_Jersey_State_House "New Jersey State House"), topped by its golden dome, alongside the [Delaware River](/wiki/Delaware_River "Delaware River")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1f/2024-04-26_13_31_07_View_northwest_along_Neilson_Street_towards_downtown_New_Brunswick%2C_New_Jersey.jpg/250px-2024-04-26_13_31_07_View_northwest_along_Neilson_Street_towards_downtown_New_Brunswick%2C_New_Jersey.jpg)](/wiki/File:2024-04-26_13_31_07_View_northwest_along_Neilson_Street_towards_downtown_New_Brunswick,_New_Jersey.jpg)

Downtown [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick,_New_Jersey "New Brunswick, New Jersey"), an educational and cultural hub of Central Jersey

[Central Jersey](/wiki/Central_Jersey "Central Jersey") is the middle portion of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). It generally comprises the following counties:

* [Middlesex County](/wiki/Middlesex_County,_New_Jersey "Middlesex County, New Jersey")
* [Mercer County](/wiki/Mercer_County,_New_Jersey "Mercer County, New Jersey")
* [Monmouth County](/wiki/Monmouth_County,_New_Jersey "Monmouth County, New Jersey")
* [Ocean County](/wiki/Ocean_County,_New_Jersey "Ocean County, New Jersey") (anything north of [Toms River](/wiki/Toms_River,_New_Jersey "Toms River, New Jersey"))
* [Union County](/wiki/Union_County,_New_Jersey "Union County, New Jersey") (anything south of [Scotch Plains Township](/wiki/Scotch_Plains_Township,_New_Jersey "Scotch Plains Township, New Jersey"), [Clark Township](/wiki/Clark_Township,_New_Jersey "Clark Township, New Jersey"), and [Linden](/wiki/Linden,_New_Jersey "Linden, New Jersey"))
* [Hunterdon County](/wiki/Hunterdon_County,_New_Jersey "Hunterdon County, New Jersey") (anything south of [Readington Township](/wiki/Readington_Township,_New_Jersey "Readington Township, New Jersey"))
* [Somerset County](/wiki/Somerset_County,_New_Jersey "Somerset County, New Jersey") (anything south of [Bridgewater Township](/wiki/Bridgewater_Township,_New_Jersey "Bridgewater Township, New Jersey"))

Notable municipalities in the region include [Trenton](/wiki/Trenton,_New_Jersey "Trenton, New Jersey") (the [state capital](/wiki/State_capital "State capital") of New Jersey and the only U.S. state capital within the New York metropolitan area), [Princeton](/wiki/Princeton,_New_Jersey "Princeton, New Jersey") (home to the [Ivy League](/wiki/Ivy_League "Ivy League") [Princeton University](/wiki/Princeton_University "Princeton University")), [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick,_New_Jersey "New Brunswick, New Jersey") (home to [Rutgers University](/wiki/Rutgers_University "Rutgers University")'s main [New Brunswick campus](/wiki/Rutgers_University%E2%80%93New_Brunswick "Rutgers University–New Brunswick"), the largest university campus in New Jersey), [Lakewood](/wiki/Lakewood_Township,_New_Jersey "Lakewood Township, New Jersey") (home to [Beth Medrash Govoha](/wiki/Beth_Medrash_Govoha "Beth Medrash Govoha"), the largest [yeshiva](/wiki/Yeshiva "Yeshiva") outside of [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel")[[95]](#cite_note-SL2017-97)), and [Edison](/wiki/Edison,_New_Jersey "Edison, New Jersey") (home to [Thomas Edison](/wiki/Thomas_Edison "Thomas Edison")'s original research laboratory, located in what is now [Edison State Park](/wiki/Edison_State_Park "Edison State Park") in [Menlo Park](/wiki/Menlo_Park,_New_Jersey "Menlo Park, New Jersey"), where inventions such as the [phonograph](/wiki/Phonograph "Phonograph"), the [motion-picture camera](/wiki/Motion-picture_camera "Motion-picture camera"), and the [incandescent light bulb](/wiki/Incandescent_light_bulb "Incandescent light bulb") were developed[[96]](#cite_note-Walsh-98)). The region also encompasses a significant portion of the [Jersey Shore](/wiki/Jersey_Shore "Jersey Shore"), including the cities of [Red Bank](/wiki/Red_Bank,_New_Jersey "Red Bank, New Jersey"), [Long Branch](/wiki/Long_Branch,_New_Jersey "Long Branch, New Jersey"), and [Asbury Park](/wiki/Asbury_Park,_New_Jersey "Asbury Park, New Jersey"). Major transportation links in Central Jersey include the [New Jersey Turnpike](/wiki/New_Jersey_Turnpike "New Jersey Turnpike"), the [Garden State Parkway](/wiki/Garden_State_Parkway "Garden State Parkway"), [US 1](/wiki/U.S._Route_1_in_New_Jersey "U.S. Route 1 in New Jersey"), [US 9](/wiki/U.S._Route_9_in_New_Jersey "U.S. Route 9 in New Jersey"), and the [Northeast Corridor](/wiki/Northeast_Corridor "Northeast Corridor"). All of these aforementioned routes bisect each other in the bustling suburb and commercial hub of [Woodbridge](/wiki/Woodbridge_Township,_New_Jersey "Woodbridge Township, New Jersey").

The beach in [Belmar, New Jersey](/wiki/Belmar,_New_Jersey "Belmar, New Jersey"), on the [Jersey Shore](/wiki/Jersey_Shore "Jersey Shore")

### Western Connecticut

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Western Connecticut")]

Main articles: [Fairfield County, Connecticut](/wiki/Fairfield_County,_Connecticut "Fairfield County, Connecticut"); [New Haven County, Connecticut](/wiki/New_Haven_County,_Connecticut "New Haven County, Connecticut"); and [Litchfield County, Connecticut](/wiki/Litchfield_County,_Connecticut "Litchfield County, Connecticut")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/52/MohawkMountain.jpg/250px-MohawkMountain.jpg)](/wiki/File:MohawkMountain.jpg)

[Mohawk Mountain Ski Area](/wiki/Mohawk_Mountain_Ski_Area "Mohawk Mountain Ski Area") in [Cornwall](/wiki/Cornwall,_Connecticut "Cornwall, Connecticut"), in the [Berkshire Mountains](/wiki/Berkshire_Mountains "Berkshire Mountains")

Fairfield, New Haven, and Litchfield counties in western Connecticut (like the state in general) are known for affluence. Large businesses are scattered throughout the area, mostly in Fairfield County. The land is flat along the coast with low hills eventually giving way to larger hills such as [The Berkshires](/wiki/The_Berkshires "The Berkshires") further inland, to the [Massachusetts](/wiki/Massachusetts "Massachusetts") border. Most of the largest cities in the state are in New Haven County (home to [Yale University](/wiki/Yale_University "Yale University")) and Fairfield County.

[Candlewood Lake](/wiki/Candlewood_Lake "Candlewood Lake") is the largest recreational lake in the New York metropolitan area. The lake is located within the [Greater Danbury](/wiki/Greater_Danbury "Greater Danbury") region, and is home to many second homes of New York City residents.

### Pike County, Pennsylvania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Pike County, Pennsylvania")]

Main article: [Pike County, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pike_County,_Pennsylvania "Pike County, Pennsylvania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d3/Dingmansfalls.jpg/250px-Dingmansfalls.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dingmansfalls.jpg)

[Dingmans Falls](/wiki/Dingmans_Falls "Dingmans Falls") in the [Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area](/wiki/Delaware_Water_Gap_National_Recreation_Area "Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area") in [Pike County, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pike_County,_Pennsylvania "Pike County, Pennsylvania") in [Northeastern Pennsylvania](/wiki/Northeastern_Pennsylvania "Northeastern Pennsylvania")

[Pike County, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pike_County,_Pennsylvania "Pike County, Pennsylvania") is located in [Northeastern Pennsylvania](/wiki/Northeastern_Pennsylvania "Northeastern Pennsylvania"). As of the 2010 Census, the population was 57,369.[[97]](#cite_note-99) Its [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") is [Milford](/wiki/Milford,_Pennsylvania "Milford, Pennsylvania").[[98]](#cite_note-100) Part of the [Pocono Mountains](/wiki/Pocono_Mountains "Pocono Mountains") region lies within Pike County, which has ranked among the fastest-growing counties of Pennsylvania.[[99]](#cite_note-101)

This county was detached from the New York-Newark-Jersey City MSA in September 2023[[100]](#cite_note-Pike-102) and now forms the [Hemlock Farms, PA μSA](/wiki/Pennsylvania_statistical_areas#Table "Pennsylvania statistical areas").

### Urban areas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Urban areas")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/New_York_metro_urban_areas.svg/250px-New_York_metro_urban_areas.svg.png)](/wiki/File:New_York_metro_urban_areas.svg)

Urban areas within the New York City combined statistical area as of the 2020 census. At the core of the New York combined statistical area (CSA) lies the New York–[Jersey City](/wiki/Jersey_City,_New_Jersey "Jersey City, New Jersey")–[Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_Jersey "Newark, New Jersey"), NY–NJ [urban area](/wiki/Urban_area "Urban area"), the largest in the United States by both area and population. Within the boundaries of the CSA the Census Bureau defines 32 other urban areas as well, some forming the core of their own metropolitan areas not within the New York metropolitan statistical area.[[106]](#cite_note-109) Urban areas situated primarily outside the New York metropolitan statistical area but within the CSA are identified with a cross (†).
